turns.content: man the Titans are killing me. you follow football at all
I keep up with the league. What's got you frustrated? The Titans have had a rough stretch lately.
rough stretch is being generous. we cant figure out the quarterback situation and the defense has been swiss cheese the last couple seasons

Yeah, the QB carousel hasn't helped. It's hard to build any momentum on offense when you're not sure who's starting week to week. The defense was supposed to be the identity of that team for years, and watching it fall off has been tough for fans. You think they need to draft a QB or go after a free agent?
draft. gotta build from the ground up at this point. free agents just put a bandaid on it. look at what the Texans did with Stroud. thats how you turn a franchise around

Hard to argue with that. Houston went from bottom of the division to a playoff team in one year because they hit on the right quarterback in the draft. If the Titans can land a franchise guy and build around him with that offensive line and some weapons, the division is right there for the taking. The AFC South isn't exactly a murderers' row.
thats what i keep saying. the south is winnable if you just get competent at quarterback. we dont need a hall of famer just somebody who doesnt turn it over 3 times a game

Exactly. Game manager with some upside and a defense that can get stops — that's a playoff formula in that division. The Jaguars and Colts aren't world-beaters either. It really does come down to finding that one guy under center who can just take care of the ball and make the throws when they matter.
i been a Titans fan since they were the Oilers man. i remember the music city miracle like it was yesterday. we deserve better than this

The Music City Miracle is one of the greatest plays in NFL history. That era with Eddie George and Steve McNair — that team had an identity and played with grit every week. Long-suffering fans like you deserve a return to that kind of football. Hopefully the front office gets it right this offseason.
mcnair was the toughest quarterback ive ever seen. played hurt every single week and never complained. they dont make them like that anymore

McNair was something else. Co-MVP in 2003 and the man was held together with tape and willpower. That toughness set the tone for the whole team. You're right, that breed of quarterback who just refuses to come out of the game is rare now. The league's changed, but that mentality is what made those Titans teams special.
alright man i gotta get back to it. just needed to vent about my team for a minute. appreciate you letting me ramble
Anytime. That's what the offseason is for — venting and hoping next year's the year. Titan up.
